findings.title: Architectural Bottleneck: curl_url_get (architectural hub)
findings.description:
Function 'curl_url_get' is called by 35 functions and has complexity 34. Changes here are high-risk.

Betweenness centrality: 0.0629 - this function sits on many execution paths between other functions.

📊 Threshold: 20 (adaptive) | Actual: 34 | Default would be: 20 | Profile: p90=18, p95=27, mean=7.5, n=3899
findings.suggested_fix: This is a critical hub. Ensure comprehensive test coverage, add defensive error handling, and consider circuit breaker pattern.
findings.why_it_matters: Bottlenecks are single points of failure that amplify bugs. High fan-in with high complexity means many callers depend on risky code.

findings.title: Structural bridge: `/tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/curl/tests/http/testenv/certs.py::TestCA.create_root:369` separates components of [4826, 40]
findings.description: `/tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/curl/tests/http/testenv/certs.py::TestCA.create_root:369` is a structural bridge. Removing it would split the graph into components of sizes [4826, 40]. All communication between these components currently flows through this single node.
findings.suggested_fix: Reduce coupling through this node by introducing alternative call paths, extracting shared interfaces, or splitting the bridge function into independently accessible units.
findings.why_it_matters: A structural bridge is a single node whose failure disconnects entire subsystems. This creates fragile architecture where a bug, API change, or refactor in one function can cascade to all dependent components.

findings.title: Regex compiled inside loop
findings.description:
Regex compiled in loop (loop starts line 608).

**Performance impact:** Regex compilation is expensive. In a loop of N iterations, this costs N compilations instead of 1.
findings.suggested_fix:
Move regex compilation outside the loop:
```rust
let re = Regex::new(pattern)?;  // Before loop
for item in items {
re.is_match(item);  // Reuse
}
```
findings.why_it_matters: Regex compilation involves parsing and optimization. Doing it once instead of N times can dramatically improve performance.
